d_notes.title: Designer's Notes
d_notes.description: This portfolio was designed to balance contemporary web presentation with subtle references to the early visual language of the web. The vertical navigation and typewriter effect are intentional symbolic choices that connect interface history, personal beginnings in web design, and a more handcrafted digital experience.

gallery.title: Initial Portfolio Sketch
gallery.description: This first sketch explored the overall layout direction, hierarchy, and relationship between navigation, hero content, and project sections.

gallery.title: Layout Variations
gallery.description: These early homepage variations explored a section-based flow where users could move quickly between Projects, Skills, and Contact without unnecessary page changes.

gallery.title: Interaction Concept
gallery.description: The site was conceived as a portfolio that feels both editorial and interactive. The goal was to create a memorable browsing experience while still keeping the structure clear and functional.

gallery.title: Visual Design & Implementation Flow
gallery.description: This diagram represents the workflow from early visual ideation in Figma to coded implementation and deployment.

projects.title: J&G Photography
projects.subtitle: Custom Web Design
projects.summary: Interactive portfolio website for a photography business with custom-coded sections and service presentation.
projects.description: Modern, interactive, and customizable website built with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and Bootstrap. Features include a dynamic navigation menu with scroll-based styling, a glass-effect hamburger menu, a custom service package builder using JSON and dropdowns, lightweight FormSubmit integration for messaging, and a Google Maps iframe for location.
projects.challenge: The site needed to present multiple photography services in a visually engaging way while still being easy to navigate for potential clients.
projects.solution: A custom front-end structure was built to combine visual impact with practical booking-oriented content. The final result emphasized flexibility, clarity, and stronger visual identity.
projects.impact: The website helped transform the business into a more polished digital presence with clearer service communication and better visual consistency.
captions: Homepage hero with dynamic top menu
Hamburger menu with glass blur effect
Generated message ready to send via FormSubmit
Embedded Google Maps for studio location

projects.title: Handyman DMD
projects.subtitle: Business Website
projects.summary: Service-based website focused on clarity, trust, and accessible information for home repair customers.
projects.description: Development of a simple and user-friendly website for Handyman DMD, designed to communicate services clearly and present the business as trustworthy and practical. The site organizes service categories, company information, and contact access in a direct structure tailored to small business needs.
projects.challenge: The business needed a professional online presence without overcomplicating the user experience or making the site feel generic.
projects.solution: A WordPress-based website was built with a clean structure, practical copy direction, and selective customization to make the site feel more intentional and business-focused.
projects.impact: The final result gave the business a stronger digital identity and a clearer way to communicate services online.

projects.title: Aurora Wellness Center
projects.subtitle: WordPress Redesign
projects.summary: Brand-aligned redesign focused on creating a calmer and more flexible digital experience for a wellness-centered business.
projects.description: This website was initially created on WordPress.com as part of a class assignment, which limited customization options. It was later migrated to WordPress.org using a custom domain, allowing greater control over layout, branding, and structure. The redesign focused on improving flexibility and aligning the site more closely with the visual identity of Aurora.
projects.challenge: The first version felt constrained by platform limitations and did not fully reflect the desired brand atmosphere.
projects.solution: The site was migrated and reworked to allow more control over the header, layout, styling, and overall experience, creating a cleaner and more cohesive design system.
projects.impact: The redesign improved both visual consistency and creative freedom, resulting in a more intentional and polished site.

projects.title: Quill Pen Press
projects.subtitle: Brand + Website
projects.summary: Identity and website concept for a publishing brand aimed at emerging writers seeking professional presentation.
projects.description: Quill Pen Press targeted emerging writers who wanted their books professionally bound and promoted after a selection process. The company was new, had few clients, no website, and strong competition. The solution was to create a professional and approachable identity using navy blue, gold, and a soft neutral background, then apply that direction in a responsive Webflow website.
projects.challenge: The brand needed credibility, personality, and a stronger visual presence in order to compete within a crowded publishing space.
projects.solution: A visual system was created around trust, professionalism, and warmth, using a refined color palette, a stylized logo, and a polished website structure to support the brand message.
projects.impact: The project demonstrates how branding and digital presentation can work together to build confidence around a new business concept.

projects.title: JLPrezc Portfolio
projects.subtitle: Photography Portfolio Website
projects.summary: Dark, image-focused portfolio for a product photographer.
projects.description: This project was developed for a photography student who needed a portfolio capable of displaying a large volume of work without relying on printed material. The goal was to create a strong first impression while showcasing both identity and photographic style. The design uses a dark theme to reinforce a dramatic, masculine, and professional tone aligned with the photographer’s style.
projects.challenge: The site needed to show many photographs clearly while still giving visitors a sense of the photographer’s identity and specialties.
projects.solution: A dark visual system was used together with large image-focused layouts, a Pinterest-style gallery, category-based navigation, and a dedicated contact section to keep the experience immersive but functional.
projects.impact: The result gives the photographer a way to present his work professionally online, even when printed presentation is limited.

projects.title: Juicy Splash
projects.subtitle: Branding + Web Concept
projects.summary: Playful tropical juice brand focused on interaction and a youthful visual identity.
projects.description: Juicy Splash was a branding project for an Advertising Design class focused on a natural juice company. The concept explored a tropical, summer-inspired approach using bright fruit-based colors such as yellows, greens, and blues to evoke freshness, islands, and beach culture. The target audience was younger, so the design was intentionally more playful and memorable.
projects.challenge: The project needed to communicate a clear brand identity while also feeling fresh, youthful, and different from a static promotional page.
projects.solution: The visual system combined tropical colors, playful energy, and interactive bubbles to make the site feel lively and engaging. Sections such as nutritional information and location were included to make the concept feel more complete and realistic.
projects.impact: The final concept shows how interaction and visual branding can work together to make a product site more memorable for a young audience.

projects.title: Berry Sweet
projects.subtitle: UI Prototype
projects.summary: Sweet-themed UI concept for a dessert gifting business.
projects.description: Berry Sweet was designed in Figma as a UI prototype for a small company focused on chocolate-covered strawberries and fruit gifts for special occasions such as birthdays and celebrations. Because the service is visually driven, the design placed strong emphasis on showing results and helping users imagine the final gift experience.
projects.challenge: The interface needed to communicate sweetness, celebration, and product appeal while still being clear and easy to browse.
projects.solution: A pink-centered visual language, image-focused layout, and desktop/mobile prototype structure were used to build a concept that feels decorative yet organized.
projects.impact: The project demonstrates the ability to design emotionally driven interfaces for gift-oriented businesses, even in a prototype stage.
